political.s: Encompass Health shows a right-leaning political donation pattern, with 72% of contributions directed to Republicans (13,000 of 18,000 disclosed). The company spent $612,000 on lobbying in 2024 and $800,000 year-to-date in 2025, focusing on healthcare industry policy issues.

charity.s: Encompass Health demonstrates moderate charitable commitment through partnerships with stroke awareness organizations and employee-supported initiatives. The company focuses on rehabilitation-related healthcare, human services, education, and arts/culture, with specific emphasis on American Stroke Association partnership and community engagement programs.
charity.focus: Rehabilitation healthcare, stroke awareness, human services, education, arts/culture, employee disaster relief (C.A.R.E. Fund)

environment.s: Encompass Health shows limited environmental commitment with no documented climate targets, emissions data, or sustainability certifications. The company has not disclosed carbon reduction initiatives or science-based targets aligned with SBTi guidelines.

labor.s: Encompass Health is ranked as one of Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For, yet faces mixed employment practices. The company has experienced sexual harassment complaints and shows significant reliance on part-time workforce (over one-third part-time), which impacts compensation equity.

dei.s: Encompass Health maintains an active DEI program with four strategic focus areas: workforce diversity, patient experience, supplier diversity, and community partnerships. The company reports increasing favorability on DEI metrics (83% in 2021 vs 77% in 2019) but lacks comprehensive EEO-1 data disclosure requested by shareholders.

animals.s: Not applicable to inpatient rehabilitation hospital services.

guns.s: Not applicable to inpatient rehabilitation hospital services.

privacy.s: Encompass Health experienced a significant data breach in 2026 affecting patient rehabilitation records across 150+ facilities, exposing names, SSNs, medical records, and discharge summaries. While the company has implemented HIPAA compliance infrastructure and NIST-based risk management systems, the breach indicates inadequate security practices for sensitive patient data.

execPay.s: CEO Mark Tarr earned $9.3 million in total compensation for 2024, with a CEO-to-median-employee pay ratio of 134:1 (earning $4.9 million vs. median employee pay of $36,707). The board approved 2025 increases including a $50,000 raise in aircraft allowance, demonstrating significant executive-to-worker pay disparity.
